hero.title: Moss: The Forgotten Relic Wiki
hero.description: Guide Quill and the Reader through a living fantasy storybook filled with environmental puzzles, hidden secrets, light combat, and two connected adventures.

gameFeature.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic
gameFeature.description: A storybook puzzle adventure that combines Moss, the Twilight Garden content and Moss: Book II into one non-VR release for Steam, PlayStation 5, Xbox and both Nintendo Switch generations.

releaseDatePricePlatforms.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic Release Date, Price and Platforms
releaseDatePricePlatforms.intro: Moss: The Forgotten Relic launches on July 16, 2026 for $19.99 across PC, PlayStation, Xbox and both Nintendo Switch generations. The flat-screen reimagining requires no VR headset and combines Moss, the Twilight Garden content and Moss: Book II.

platforms.name: Steam
platforms.details: Single-player with controller and mouse-and-keyboard support, Steam achievements, Steam Cloud and Family Sharing.

platforms.name: Steam Deck
platforms.details: No separate handheld edition is required. The standard Steam purchase can be played on Steam Deck.

platforms.name: PlayStation 5
platforms.details: Features redesigned controls, a new camera presentation and enhanced visuals. PlayStation VR is not required.

platforms.name: Xbox One
platforms.details: The Xbox listing includes Smart Delivery, Xbox achievements and Xbox cloud saves.

platforms.name: Xbox Series X|S
platforms.details: Xbox lists 4K Ultra HD, 60 fps+, Smart Delivery, Xbox Play Anywhere, achievements and cloud saves.

platforms.name: Nintendo Switch
platforms.details: Nintendo Switch owners receive a free upgrade to the Nintendo Switch 2 version at no additional cost.

platforms.name: Nintendo Switch 2
platforms.details: Available as a native Switch 2 release and as a free upgrade for owners of the Nintendo Switch version.

beginnerGuide.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Learn the dual-control system, solve the first environmental puzzles and survive early Arcane encounters. These steps focus on Book I and avoid major story spoilers.

steps.title: Start with Book I
steps.description: The main menu allows access to Book I or Book II, but Book I introduces Quill, the Reader and the game's puzzle language in the intended story order.
steps.tip: Book II includes a recap and control refresher, but its puzzles and combat become more demanding.

steps.title: Separate Quill and Reader Controls
steps.description: Use Quill's controls for running, jumping, climbing, dodging and sword attacks. Use the Reader cursor and interaction input to grab blocks, operate platforms, pull mechanisms and hold Arcane enemies.
steps.tip: Before attempting a busy room, identify which actions belong to Quill and which belong to the Reader.

steps.title: Read the Environment
steps.description: Look for scratch marks on ledges, walls and movable surfaces. These visual marks commonly indicate where Quill can climb or where an object must be positioned.
steps.tip: Check foreground corners, upper ledges and routes partly hidden behind scenery.

steps.title: Build the Route First
steps.description: Move blocks beneath ledges, align platforms and activate mechanisms before sending Quill across. Preparing the entire route reduces failed jumps and unnecessary resets.
steps.tip: If a platform returns to its original position, keep holding it with the Reader while Quill crosses.

steps.title: Use Arcane Enemies as Puzzle Tools
steps.description: The Reader can hold or control beetle-like Arcane enemies. Move them onto pressure plates, use them to trigger switches or keep them still while Quill attacks.
steps.tip: Do not defeat every enemy immediately because some puzzles require an active Arcane unit.

steps.title: Fight, Dodge and Heal
steps.description: Use short sword combinations, dodge away from visible attacks and create space before healing Quill through the Reader interaction. A Skip Combat option is also available during combat sections and boss encounters.
steps.tip: Hold nearby enemies with the Reader or move Quill to a safe corner before healing.

steps.title: Collect Scrolls and Relic Dust
steps.description: Search optional routes for scrolls and break barrels, crates and pots for Relic Dust. Scrolls unlock stained-glass artwork, while Relic Dust becomes important for special areas and optional content in Book II.
steps.tip: The game autosaves regularly, and falling or being defeated usually returns Quill to the latest checkpoint or the beginning of the room.

walkthroughChapterOrder.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic Walkthrough and Chapter Order
walkthroughChapterOrder.intro: Follow the intended Book I, Twilight Garden and Book II route without revealing major endings. Each section lists its chapters, main objective and important mechanics.

sections.title: Book I Opening
sections.objective: Meet Quill, establish the Reader bond, return toward her home and begin the journey to find Sir Argus.
sections.mechanics: Basic movement, climbing, jumping, movable blocks, simple switches, healing and early sword combat.
sections.spoilerNote: Covers the story setup without revealing major encounters.

sections.title: Book I Temples and Ruins
sections.objective: Cross increasingly dangerous ruins, solve multi-stage platform puzzles and use Arcane enemies to operate mechanisms.
sections.mechanics: Raising and rotating platforms, pressure plates, controlled Arcane enemies, timed crossings and collectible routes.
sections.spoilerNote: These chapters contain the three portal opportunities connected to Twilight Garden.

sections.title: Twilight Garden Side Adventure
sections.objective: Enter the garden through all three portals, complete its trials and earn equipment and abilities that reveal hidden paths.
sections.mechanics: Standalone challenge rooms, new puzzle interactions, hidden-path revelation and upgraded combat tools.
sections.spoilerNote: The portals can be completed in any order, but entering them when first encountered keeps the side adventure aligned with Book I.

sections.title: Book I Finale
sections.objective: Push through the final approach, combine Quill and Reader abilities and resolve the first book's rescue mission.
sections.mechanics: Faster platforming, larger combat rooms, chase sequences and advanced Reader-assisted puzzles.
sections.spoilerNote: Boss and ending details are hidden.

sections.title: Book II First Half
sections.objective: Continue after Book I, gather allies and Glass-related leads, and unlock new ways to reshape routes through the environment.
sections.mechanics: Nature Attunement for growing vines, larger dioramas, advanced Arcane manipulation and expanded objectives.
sections.spoilerNote: Book II begins with a story recap and a short control refresher.

sections.title: Book II Second Half
sections.objective: Secure the remaining tools and allies, cross the late-game regions and complete Quill's campaign against the Arcane threat.
sections.mechanics: Charged sword traversal, chakram switches, hammer puzzles, character switching, mirror routing and harder boss encounters.
sections.spoilerNote: Final character outcomes and the ending are not described.

puzzleSolutions.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic Puzzle Solutions
puzzleSolutions.intro: Use these spoiler-light solutions for puzzle types repeated across both books. Each guide explains the required mechanic without revealing major story events.

steps: Find the ledge marked with visible scratches.
Use the Reader cursor to grab the highlighted block or statue.
Drag it directly beneath the ledge and release it on a flat surface.
Move Quill onto the block and jump or climb onto the ledge.
puzzles.tip: If Quill cannot climb, adjust the block until its top edge sits close to the scratched ledge.

steps: Move Quill onto the safe starting platform.
Grab the movable platform with the Reader.
Raise, slide or rotate it until it connects the two walkable edges.
Keep holding the mechanism if it resets automatically.
Move Quill across before releasing the platform.
puzzles.tip: Some rooms require moving the same platform twice: once to carry Quill and again to create the exit route.

puzzles.puzzle: Arcane Enemy on a Pressure Plate
steps: Leave at least one beetle-like Arcane enemy alive.
Stun or grab it with the Reader interaction.
Drag the enemy onto the pressure plate or target mechanism.
Hold it in place until the gate, bridge or platform finishes moving.
Send Quill through before defeating or releasing the enemy.
puzzles.tip: If the mechanism turns off, center the enemy on the plate or continue holding it with the Reader.

puzzles.puzzle: Timed Switch with Quill and an Arcane
steps: Place Quill beside the route that opens when the switch is active.
Control an Arcane enemy with the Reader and move it toward the switch.
Trigger the switch with the enemy.
Immediately guide Quill through the opened route while maintaining Reader control.
Release the enemy after Quill reaches a safe platform.
puzzles.tip: Position Quill before activating the switch so no time is wasted.

steps: Look for a dead end, unreachable collectible or incomplete route.
Use the acquired reveal interaction on the nearby marked area.
Wait for the hidden glass platform or pathway to appear.
Move Quill across and search the surrounding area for optional collectibles.
puzzles.tip: Twilight Garden rewards and later abilities reveal paths that could not be accessed earlier.

steps: Locate the dormant plant or disconnected vine near the destination.
Use the Reader's Nature Attunement interaction to grow or redirect it.
Connect the vine to a wall, ledge or second plant until it forms a continuous route.
Guide Quill onto the vine and climb to the next platform.
puzzles.tip: If the vine stops short, look for another plant anchor or approach it from the opposite side.

steps: Identify whether the target is distant, heavy or connected to a traversal gap.
Use the charged sword ability for dash-based gaps and sword-related mechanisms.
Use the chakram when a switch must be struck from range.
Use the hammer on heavy objects and mechanisms that ignore lighter attacks.
Complete the opened route before the mechanism resets.
puzzles.tip: The target's distance, weight and shape usually indicate which weapon is required.

steps: Find the light source, each movable mirror and the final receiver.
Rotate the mirror closest to the source first.
Follow the beam and adjust each mirror in order.
Continue until the beam reaches the receiver.
Move Quill after the connected mechanism or route activates.
puzzles.tip: Work from the light source toward the receiver so the incorrectly aligned mirror is easy to identify.

scrollsDustSecrets.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic Scrolls, Dust and Secrets
scrollsDustSecrets.intro: Track Forgotten Fragments, Relic Dust, stained-glass rewards, Twilight Garden portals and secret armor areas across both books.

groups.label: Book I
groups.title: Forgotten Fragment Scrolls
groups.target: 31 scroll fragments across six main chapters
checklist: Inspect rooftops, rear ledges and platforms positioned above the normal route.
Move Reader-controlled blocks into alternate positions to reach optional ledges.
Look behind buildings, gates, statues and foreground scenery before changing rooms.
Collecting the scroll fragments restores the stained-glass artwork shown between chapters.

groups.label: Book I
groups.title: Twilight Garden Secrets
checklist: Search the side of the large gate in The Mire Temple.
Check behind the pillars near the beginning of Last Respite.
Explore the side of the town shortly before entering the pub in The Abandoned City.
Completing the connected Twilight Garden route adds optional puzzles, equipment rewards and the Recast Commander encounter.

groups.label: Book II
groups.title: Stained-Glass Fragment Sets
groups.target: 48 Forgotten Fragments, with eight fragments assigned to each of six stained-glass areas
checklist: Return to earlier rooms after obtaining Nature Attunement, Sword Dash, the Charged Chakram and Cariel's Hammer.
Use growing vines and root bridges to reach high or previously disconnected platforms.
Reveal hidden glass platforms and inspect routes concealed behind foreground structures.
Use the hammer on armored mechanisms and heavy obstacles blocking collectible paths.

groups.label: Book II
groups.title: Relic Dust and Secret Armor
groups.target: Break containers and open all three Relic Shrines
checklist: Break every barrel, crate, pot and destructible container with Quill or the Reader.
Check both sides of bridges, elevators and room transitions because dust containers can sit outside the main camera focus.
Use collected dust to open sealed Relic Shrine doors.
Explore each shrine completely to obtain the optional armor sets stored inside.

groups.label: Progress Check
groups.title: How to Confirm Missing Collectibles
groups.target: Review progress before repeating complete sections
checklist: For Book I, open Chapter Select and inspect the collectible progress shown for each chapter.
For Book II, use the Objectives screen to review fragments, dust and other unfinished goals.
Finish the story before final cleanup so every traversal ability is available.
Revisit rooms containing unreachable ledges, inactive mechanisms, glass platforms or sealed shrine doors.

weaponsAbilities.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic Weapons and Abilities
weaponsAbilities.intro: Learn how the sword, chakram, hammer and Reader-assisted abilities connect combat, environmental puzzles and collectible backtracking.

items.name: Sword
items.availability: Quill's primary weapon throughout both books
items.combatUse: Fast close-range swings and sweeping attacks against standard Arcane enemies.
items.abilityUnlock: Unlocked through a Relic Shrine in the Conservatory during Book II.
items.abilityUse: Charge the sword with Reader assistance, then dash through enemies or across long gaps.
items.explorationUse: Cross marked gaps, cut ropes and reach fragments that were inaccessible during the first visit.

items.name: Chakram
items.availability: Received during Book II through Quill's journey with Sahima
items.combatUse: Strike distant enemies and hit targets on both the outward throw and returning path.
items.abilityUnlock: Activated with Reader assistance after the weapon is obtained.
items.abilityUse: Throw the charged weapon into distant surfaces or mechanisms, then recall it when needed.
items.explorationUse: Open remote passages, trigger unreachable objects and clear routes that cannot be reached with the sword.

items.name: Cariel's Hammer
items.availability: Obtained in the Foundry during Book II
items.combatUse: Slow, powerful strikes that are effective against armored Forged enemies.
items.abilityUnlock: Charge the hammer and let the Reader position its magical impact area.
items.abilityUse: Trigger the suspended hammer effect when enemies or boss weak points enter the marked zone.
items.explorationUse: Smash machinery, armored barriers and heavy mechanisms required for later collectibles.

items.name: Nature Attunement
items.availability: Acquired in the Sprite Realm during Book II
items.combatUse: Primarily an exploration ability rather than a direct attack.
items.abilityUnlock: Granted through the Sprite Realm story route.
items.abilityUse: Grow climbable ivy, extend plant paths and connect roots across gaps.
items.explorationUse: Creates new vertical routes and allows several earlier areas to be completed during backtracking.

items.name: Glass Platform Reveal
items.availability: Introduced as Book II expands Quill and the Reader's abilities
items.combatUse: Helps reposition Quill around encounters containing hidden or magical terrain.
items.abilityUnlock: Used as later areas introduce glass-based paths and puzzles.
items.abilityUse: Expose platforms that are invisible or unusable during an earlier visit.
items.explorationUse: Opens optional routes leading to scroll fragments, shrine paths and secret areas.

items.name: Reader Interaction
items.availability: Available throughout the adventure and expanded during Book II
items.combatUse: Stun or control enemies, reposition threats and heal Quill when she is out of immediate danger.
items.abilityUnlock: Introduced during the opening tutorials of each book.
items.abilityUse: Move blocks, rotate platforms, pull mechanisms, break containers and control selected Arcane enemies.
items.explorationUse: Place enemies on pressure plates, aim their attacks at switches and create safe paths for Quill.

bossesCombatGuide.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic Bosses and Combat Guide
bossesCombatGuide.intro: Use Quill's dodges and weapons together with Reader healing, enemy control and environmental interactions to clear major encounters.

bosses.title: Arcane and Forged Battle Rooms
bosses.encounter: Mixed groups of Screechers, Tickers, Bombers, Scorchers and armored Forged enemies appear throughout both books.
attackPatterns: Melee enemies rush or swipe when Quill remains close.
Ranged enemies fire toward Quill's current position.
Explosive enemies can damage nearby enemies when controlled or redirected.
strategy: Dodge sideways rather than retreating into walls or foreground scenery.
Defeat ranged attackers first when they limit Quill's movement.
Use the Reader to stun, hold or reposition one enemy while Quill attacks another.
Use controlled enemies to activate switches, block attacks or damage other Forged units.
bosses.readerRole: Control dangerous enemies, trigger charged weapon effects and heal Quill after moving her away from active attacks.
bosses.skipCombat: Optional battle rooms can be bypassed through the encounter's Skip Combat prompt, although enemies required for puzzles still need to be manipulated.

bosses.title: Sarffog
bosses.encounter: The giant serpent serves as the climactic threat of Book I.
attackPatterns: Sarffog launches fiery attacks across Quill's route.
The encounter combines rapid climbing, platform movement and timed evasions.
Later sections require Quill to advance while the serpent pressures the available path.
strategy: Keep Quill moving and wait for each fire attack to pass before committing to the next jump.
Use the Reader to operate environmental elements immediately when the route opens.
Avoid stopping on narrow platforms where the next attack can trap Quill.
Prioritize reaching the next safe level instead of attempting unnecessary attacks.
bosses.readerRole: Maintain the escape route, protect Quill during transitions and react quickly to interactive objects in the arena.
bosses.skipCombat: The Skip Combat option can be used when the encounter prompt is available.

bosses.title: The Warden
bosses.encounter: A towering Foundry boss fought with Cariel's Hammer during Book II.
attackPatterns: The Warden marks areas before slamming its fists or heated hammer into the arena.
Forged enemies enter while the Warden prepares later attacks.
Hammer strikes release expanding waves of heat across the platform.
strategy: Move away from orange impact markers before each slam lands.
Charge Quill's hammer and position the Hammer Trap where the Warden's limb will remain exposed.
Jump over the expanding heat waves rather than attempting to outrun them.
When the Warden's hammer becomes vulnerable, use the Reader to hold it and attack its glowing weak points with Quill.
bosses.readerRole: Activate Hammer Traps, restrain the boss weapon during vulnerable phases and help control smaller Forged enemies.
bosses.skipCombat: The encounter supports the optional Skip Combat accessibility feature.

bosses.title: Tylan
bosses.encounter: The owl Tylan is the final major boss at the Pinnacle in Book II.
attackPatterns: Tylan performs repeated ground-pound attacks with a delayed final impact.
The encounter includes a fast platforming and chase section.
The final arena combines aerial attacks, environmental targets and short damage windows.
strategy: Prepare a Hammer Trap before the final ground pound in each opening sequence.
During the chase, prioritize safe landings and avoid attacking unless the route is secure.
Use the Charged Chakram on the distant magical lantern.
Use Sword Dash to cut the opposite rope, then pull the bell into Tylan's path with the Reader.
Attack rapidly while Tylan is stunned against the bell, then reset for the next cycle.
bosses.readerRole: Charge Quill's weapons, manipulate the bell and keep track of Tylan while Quill handles enemies and platforming.
bosses.skipCombat: Players may use Skip Combat if they prefer to continue the story without completing the full boss sequence.

gameLengthPerformanceAccessibility.title: Moss The Forgotten Relic Game Length, Performance and Accessibility
gameLengthPerformanceAccessibility.intro: Compare story length, collectible cleanup, handheld performance, PC features, subtitles, autosaving and Skip Combat support.

rows.notes: Covers the main story route without finding every scroll or dust container.
Book II is larger, introduces more weapons and includes more complex puzzles and boss encounters.
Completing Twilight Garden paths, Relic Shrines, armor sets, scrolls and dust cleanup adds several additional hours.
The PC version supports Steam achievements, Steam Cloud, Family Sharing and approximately 15 GB of storage.

rows.data: 60 FPS in handheld and docked modes
rows.notes: Reviews report a crisp image, fast area loading and readable handheld text. The listed Switch 2 download size is 10.3 GB.
Nintendo Switch purchasers receive access to the Switch 2 upgrade at no additional cost according to Polyarc's launch update.
Deaths and failed jumps restart from a recent checkpoint, limiting lost progress.
Steam lists ten supported interface and subtitle languages, with full voice support available in selected languages.
A prompt appears at combat encounters and can also be used during boss battles. It does not remove enemy interactions required for environmental puzzles.

rows.data: Enhanced visuals and Smart Follow Camera
rows.notes: The flatscreen edition includes reworked visuals, new handcrafted cutscenes and a camera designed to keep Quill visible without VR movement.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Moss: The Forgotten Relic

questions.question: Is Moss The Forgotten Relic a single-player game?
questions.answer: Yes. Moss: The Forgotten Relic is a single-player storybook adventure. The package combines Moss, the Twilight Garden content and Moss: Book II, and no VR headset is required.

questions.question: Do I need a VR headset to play Moss The Forgotten Relic?
questions.answer: No. Moss: The Forgotten Relic is built for standard screens with redesigned controls and a new follow camera. It runs on Steam, PlayStation 5, Xbox and both Nintendo Switch generations without any VR equipment.

questions.question: How much does Moss The Forgotten Relic cost and when does it release?
questions.answer: Moss: The Forgotten Relic releases on July 16, 2026 for $19.99. A free demo is available on Steam during Steam Next Fest so you can try the opening before launch.

questions.question: Is Moss The Forgotten Relic verified for Steam Deck?
questions.answer: Yes. The Steam version of Moss: The Forgotten Relic is officially Steam Deck Verified, and the same purchase runs on the handheld with no separate edition required.

questions.question: How long is Moss The Forgotten Relic?
questions.answer: A normal story playthrough of Moss: The Forgotten Relic takes about nine to ten hours across Book I and Book II, with extra time for scroll fragments, Relic Dust and secret armor cleanup.

questions.question: Can I skip combat in Moss The Forgotten Relic?
questions.answer: Yes. A Skip Combat prompt appears at combat encounters and during boss battles in Moss: The Forgotten Relic, while enemies required for environmental puzzles still need to be manipulated.
